case: calculator-mouse-math (speed vs accuracy comparison across 3 paths)
model: agent_click_sequence and agent_do via Electron bridge, hcompany provider, llama.cpp b9082 pinned
summary: Quantified the crop-vs-uncrop speed/accuracy tradeoff on a tiny UI (Calculator 230×408). Cropping is 3.5× faster but destroys grounding accuracy at sub-60px buttons. Shipped padding-crop fix (Option B) as the proposed compromise.

test_results.verdict: FAST but INACCURATE. 1/6 buttons. At 230×408, the model picks regions but can't distinguish individual 57px buttons. Crop only useful for large UIs.

result.comment: At full 1512×982 resolution the model has ~6× more pixels per button and grounds correctly on all 6.

test_results.verdict: ACCURATE but SLOW. 6/6 math correct. 60s wall is dominated by 4114-token prompt processing per target × 6 targets in parallel.

tradeoff_quantified.wall_ratio: 60.5 / 17.1 = 3.5× (not 23× — there's a per-call fixed overhead that doesn't shrink with tokens)
tradeoff_quantified.interpretation: For tiny UIs (Calculator-class, < 300px window), cropping makes inference fast but the model can't resolve individual UI elements. The grounding model's effective spatial resolution is ~4 pixels per normalized unit, so a 57px button gets ~14 normalized-unit-wide target — comparable to typical model jitter. Big UIs (Chrome window-class, > 800px) shouldn't have this problem.

implementations_shipped.name: raise-then-recapture
implementations_shipped.what: /window/raise bridge endpoint + raiseMacApp helper + loop calls raise+sleep+recapture before crop. Prevents Z-order occlusion where crop captures the WRONG app's pixels at the target's coords (May-11 false-positive DONE where Ponder's session UI was visible at Calculator's bounds because Calculator was buried).

implementations_shipped.name: bounds-validate clicks
implementations_shipped.what: When targetApp set, refuse to execute clicks whose post-translation coords fall outside the target window. Pushes a [note: …] to history so the brain re-grounds instead of focusing whatever's underneath.

implementations_shipped.name: Option B — window-padding crop
implementations_shipped.what: Pad the cropped region by 25% of window's min-dim (clamped [100, 250]px). For Calculator 230×408 → padded ~430×608 (~3.5× more pixels for the model). For Chrome 1200×800 → padded ~1600×1200 (adaptive, large windows get smaller relative padding). Click bounds-validation still uses the RAW window bounds — padding only widens the model's visual context.

not_tested_due_to_time: bulbasaur upload via agent_do — Chrome navigation kept failing (URL bar typing not sticking, Chrome bounds query returning menu-bar only). Setup overhead exceeded the bench budget; deferred.
Option A (upscale crop 2×) — would require client-side image scaling before sending to grounder, OR a `scale` param on the Modal endpoint. Not yet implemented.
Padding effect on Calculator math accuracy — requires Electron restart to load 73dc680.
next_action_items: 1. User restart Electron — loads commit 73dc680 (padding) + f75b00f (bounds-validation, which the bridge at 1976d6c also doesn't have). All three fixes from today's session land in one restart.

2. Re-run Calculator 47×8 via agent_do (same task as the May-11 user-supplied log). Expected: padded ~430×608 crop fires; grounding ~25-40s for 6 targets (between cropped 9.6s and uncropped 58s); math = 376; verifier verifies.
3. If accuracy is still poor on Calculator: drop to uncropped for sub-300px windows (heuristic: only crop windows >= 400px in either dimension).

4. Bulbasaur bench: pre-position Chrome with a real listing open (manual setup), then run agent_do with targetApp:"Google Chrome". Chrome's UI elements are much larger than Calculator's buttons (>= 30px text, > 50px buttons) so accuracy should be fine even uncropped.
